commit.message:
feat: add AI Agent Phase 1 MVP — 2-tier LLM pipeline for EVM attack detection

Implements the AI agent infrastructure for LLM-assisted transaction analysis:

Architecture (2-pass):
- AiJudge: screening model (fast/cheap) → deep model (escalation on high confidence)
- ContextExtractor: StepRecord traces → structured AgentContext (O(n) precompute)
- Hallucination Guard: verifies AI evidence against actual on-chain data
- CostTracker: file-based persistence with daily/monthly budget resets
- CircuitBreaker: API failure protection with cooldown auto-reset
- HourlyRateTracker: sliding-window rate limiting (immutable pattern)
- BlockConcurrencyTracker: per-block concurrent request limiting

New modules (12 files, ~4,000 LoC):
- src/sentinel/ai/{ai_config,circuit_breaker,context,cost,guard,judge,rate_limit}.rs
- src/sentinel/ai/{context_test,cost_test,guard_test,judge_test}.rs
- src/sentinel/rpc_ai.rs — AI integration helpers for RPC sentinel

Integration:
- rpc_service.rs: AI enrichment in deep analysis path (feature-gated)
- config.rs: AiConfig field on SentinelConfig
- types.rs: agent_verdict field on SentinelAlert
- cli/mod.rs: AI configuration support

Feature gate: ai_agent (opt-in, no impact on existing pipeline)
Tests: 667 passed, 0 failed, 25 ignored

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Moderation logging for audit trail
- Public tier-info endpoint

commit.message:
Release v2.5.0: Add planning rigor reference — assumptions, pre-mortem, falsification signals, prediction accuracy

New reference file with 7 epistemic techniques integrated across the protocol:
- Assumption tracking in plan.md (grounded in findings, linked to steps)
- Pre-mortem & falsification signals (merged into one section with STOP IF triggers)
- Exploration confidence gate (quality check on EXPLORE → PLAN transition)
- Prediction accuracy in verification.md (calibration data for LESSONS.md)
- Ghost constraint hunting in RE-PLAN (check if failed constraint is still valid)
- Decomposition analysis at iteration 5 (actionable sub-goals before hard stop)
- Phase balance heuristic (effort distribution guideline)

Removed vestigial "Risks" section from plan.md (subsumed by Failure Modes + Pre-Mortem).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

バグ修正: 新着確認後に再ログインすると再び新着になる問題

原因: GASはメール解析のたびに createdAt = new Date() で上書きするため、
      更新ごとに全予約の createdAt が現在時刻になり、
      allCheckedAt（確認した時刻）より未来になってしまっていた。

修正: loadFromGAS() で既存IDの createdAt は localStorage 保存値を優先し、
      新規IDのみ初回取得時刻を割り当てる。

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 경로 내부 루프 추적으로 솔리드 디스크 버그 근본 수정

Linux Autotrace는 여러 획을 M 없이 L/C로 이어 한 붓 그리기로 출력.
기존 start↔end 비교 방식은 이 경우 수백px gap으로 인해 Z 추가 불가.
→ 경로를 따라가며 현재 위치가 서브패스 시작점으로 되돌아오는 지점(내부 루프)을
  실시간으로 감지. 루프 발견 시 Z + M 삽입으로 서브패스 분리.

feat(simse-bridge,simse-tui): wire E2E ACP chat flow with claude-agent-acp

Wire the complete chat message flow through the ACP protocol:
- Add lazy ACP connection on first chat message (not on startup)
- Fix ACP protocol compliance: send protocolVersion/clientInfo in initialize,
  cwd/mcpServers in session/new, rename content→prompt in session/prompt

  nested session detection

commit.message:
Add __init__.py to all packages to fix Streamlit Cloud ImportError

Without __init__.py, Python treats auth/, config/, data/, utils/, charts/
as namespace packages. Streamlit Cloud can resolve imports inconsistently
from cached builds, causing 'cannot import name X' errors. Explicit package
markers ensure clean module resolution on every deploy.

Add cdn.jsdelivr.net to CSP connect-src for SW caching

Service worker fetches CDN scripts (Chart.js, pdf.js) via fetch()
which is governed by connect-src, not script-src. Without this the
SW cache fails on fresh installs.

  hardcoded service list — neither has a working ingress, nginx was
  returning 404 which was incorrectly counted as UP

Remove investigator sub-agent, add file logging, simplify architecture

The nested query() pattern (investigator inside orchestrator MCP handler)
caused subprocess leaks, MCP stream corruption, and memory pressure on
4GB VPS. The orchestrator now investigates directly via bash.

Changes:
- Remove _run_investigator and investigate_issue tool
- Give orchestrator query_memory tool (was investigator-only)
- Update prompt: investigate directly, aim for 15 tool calls
- Add file logging: activity logs written to alerts.log_file
- logging.basicConfig() outputs to both stderr and log file

feat: add Nullform WAF — M₁₁ algebraic firewall middleware

- Add Express middleware with two-layer defense:
  Layer 1: Immediate recon block (WP scan, .env probe, directory enum, SQLi, traversal)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(pdf): real-time progress, per-validator timing, and worker reliability fixes (#239)

* feat(pdf): async BullMQ processing for PDF accessibility audits (#236)

- Return HTTP 202 immediately on upload; worker processes in background
- Save file to permanent storage before enqueueing (retry-safe)
- BullMQ job ID = Prisma job ID for unified tracking
- In-process fallback when Redis is not configured
- Implement GET /pdf/job/:jobId/status endpoint (removes 501 stub)
- Make maxPages and maxAuditPages configurable via env vars (MAX_PDF_PAGES, MAX_AUDIT_PAGES)
- Raise upload limit to 500MB to support large documents
- Extend server timeout for large file uploads

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(pdf): parallel batch page processing for accessibility audits

Replace sequential page-by-page iteration with batched Promise.all across
all three PDF extraction services, and run text+image extraction in parallel
at the top level.

Changes:
- pdf-comprehensive-parser: run textExtractor and imageExtractor with Promise.all
- text-extractor: both font-size and text-extraction passes now process 10
  pages at a time concurrently instead of one by one
- image-extractor: process 5 pages concurrently (smaller batch, images are
  memory-intensive)
- structure-analyzer analyzeLinks(): process 10 pages concurrently; fix
  link IDs to use per-page annotation index instead of global array length

Expected speedup: ~5-8x for large PDFs (800 pages: ~30min → ~4min)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf(pdf): skip image decompression and pre-filter tiny images

Two critical fixes for math/technical PDFs with thousands of equation images:

1. Skip xObject.getContents() decompression when includeBase64=false — use
   the dict's Length entry for file size instead. This avoids decompressing
   tens of thousands of FlateDecode streams just to record metadata.

2. Pre-filter images by Width/Height from dict metadata before calling
   processImage(), so tiny images (equations, icons) are skipped entirely
   before any stream access.

Combined with the batch parallel changes, this reduces audit time for
large math/technical PDFs from 30+ min to minutes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(pdf): real-time page progress during accessibility audit

Thread a per-batch progress callback from the BullMQ worker down through
pdfAuditService → pdfComprehensiveParserService → textExtractorService.

- textExtractor emits onPageBatch(processed, total) after each 10-page batch
- comprehensiveParser calls the callback with (0, total) immediately so the
  frontend knows the page count, then forwards per-batch updates
- Worker maps page progress to 20–88% job progress range
- Worker stores totalPages in job.input on first callback so the polling
  endpoint already exposes it without schema changes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(pdf): per-validator progress callbacks during accessibility audit

Emits onValidatorComplete after each of the 4 validators (Structure & Tags,
Alt Text, Color Contrast, Tables) with label, issueCount, and completed/total
indices. Worker stores results in job.input.validatorProgress and advances
progress 88–95% as each validator finishes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(pdf): add startedAt timestamp to validator progress entries

Each validator now records startedAt (when it began) and completedAt (when
it finished) in job.input.validatorProgress, enabling per-validator duration
display in the UI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(pdf): fix audit result overwrite and add BullMQ lock duration

- Remove double DB write in accessibility processor: base.worker already
  writes COMPLETED status + output, processor no longer calls prisma.job.update
  directly (which was silently overwriting the full audit report with minimal data)
- Return full auditReport in result.data so base.worker persists it correctly
- Use job.data.tenantId/userId for AcrJob creation (avoids extra DB query)
- Add lockDuration option to createWorker (default 30s)
- Set lockDuration=10min on accessibility worker to prevent BullMQ stall
  detection for large PDF audits
- Raise accessibility worker concurrency from 2 to 5

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
